_publications._publicationTitle: Unbalancing cAMP and Ras/MAPK pathways as a therapeutic strategy for cutaneous neurofibromas.

_contextSnippet: Using S100B staining in combination with Click-iT EdU we measured the proliferation status of SC and FB populations in cocultures. S100B staining showed that, while ogerin alone elicited no apparent morphological change in SCs
details: Used in combination with Click-iT EdU to measure proliferation status of SC and FB populations in cocultures. Also used for morphological characterization showing selumetinib enhanced rectilinear and spindle shape morphology of SCs
